codeReview.summary: Reviewed the original failing feature `mini-stateful-watch-auth-shell` and the follow-up fix `mini-home-authenticated-state-honesty`. The original bug in `mini/lib/shell-surface-state.js` was real: Home treated the user as signed in only when `digestResult.ok` was true and reused signed-out copy for authenticated-empty states. The fix correctly moves the builder logic onto persisted session state, adds distinct authenticated-empty/authenticated-error copy, and updates proof/test coverage for those post-response states. However, the page lifecycle still leaves an already-mounted Home page on its previous signed-out data until `loadDigests()` resolves, so the signed-in posture can still be wrong immediately after an in-app login or tab return.

issues.description: `onShow()` only calls `refreshOverview()`, and `refreshOverview()` does not rebuild Home from `getSession()` until after awaiting `loadDigests()` at line 54. If a user signs in on Watch and returns to an already-mounted Home page, Home keeps its prior signed-out copy while the digest read is pending, so Home and Account can still disagree about signed-in posture on slow or stalled requests.

summary: Fail. The builder-level fix is real, but the Home page still has a reachable stale-state path after in-app login: an already-mounted Home surface does not switch to a signed-in loading posture until the digest request completes. The prior blocking honesty issue is therefore only partially resolved.
